outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.headline: Oil falls after Trump says U.S. will end Iran war 'very quickly,' predicts prices will plummet
outlets.summary: Trump told reporters the war 'will end very quickly' and Iran is 'desperate' — Brent eased slightly on the optimism. Analysts note the statement contradicts Araghchi's simultaneous 'cannot trust Americans' warning.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Israeli airstrikes kill 19 in southern Lebanon, including children, despite 45-day ceasefire extension
outlets.summary: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed 19, including 3 children and 4 women, at Deir Qanun al-Nahr, Nabatieh, and Kfar Sir — raising questions about the 45-day ceasefire extension announced the same day.

outlets.name: ABC News
outlets.headline: Araghchi: 'We cannot trust the Americans at all' — Iran FM issues sharpest trust-breakdown statement of war
outlets.summary: Iranian FM Araghchi declared the US sends 'different messages, sometimes two in a single day' making trust impossible — directly contradicting Trump's claim that Iran is 'desperate' for a deal.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: CENTCOM chief unloads after Democrat asks how many more Americans must die before Congress votes on war
outlets.summary: CENTCOM Commander responded sharply to a congressman questioning the war's constitutional authorization, defending the mission and highlighting successes against Iran's military.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Vance: Iran 'cannot possess a nuclear weapon' — that's the non-negotiable red line as deadline nears
outlets.summary: VP Vance elaborated on the administration's red line on Day 82: Iran 'cannot possess a nuclear weapon' is absolute, but Vance expressed optimism saying 'we've made a lot of progress.'

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Netanyahu calls security cabinet amid reports Israel and US readying Iran nuclear facility strikes
outlets.summary: Netanyahu convened the security cabinet as Trump's 2-3 day deadline ticks down, with reports of coordinated US-Israel planning for strikes on Natanz, Fordow, and Arak if diplomacy fails.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war Day 82: Trump sets Friday-Sunday deadline; Araghchi 'cannot trust Americans'; Iran releases US prisoner
outlets.summary: Trump's 'very quickly' optimism clashes with Araghchi's trust breakdown; Iran releases US resident Dalili as goodwill gesture; Netanyahu convenes security cabinet; Xi-Putin ceasefire call in Beijing.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Xi warns against resuming Iran attacks after meeting Putin in Beijing; G7 calls for Hormuz free transit
outlets.summary: Xi told Putin 'a comprehensive ceasefire is imperative' and 'restarting war is even more unacceptable' — the strongest language from Beijing yet, timed with Trump's Friday-Sunday deadline.

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: Gulf states talk Trump out of Iran strikes; Xi tells Putin it's 'imperative' to stop the Middle East conflict
outlets.summary: Simultaneous diplomatic pressure from Gulf states and China-Russia axis to prevent Trump from resuming strikes — but Araghchi's trust-breakdown statement signals Iran is not making concessions.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Netanyahu convenes security cabinet amid reports Israel, US preparing Iran strikes if talks fail
outlets.summary: Netanyahu held a security cabinet meeting and spoke with Trump as reports of coordinated nuclear facility strike preparation circulate — he maintains the war is 'not over' until enrichment stops.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: IDF officer killed in Lebanon as Israel strikes kill 19 civilians; Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days
outlets.summary: Major Itamar Sapir, 27, killed by Hezbollah fire in Qouzah. Israel simultaneously struck 25+ Hezbollah sites and 19 civilians died — highlighting the 'porous' nature of the 45-day ceasefire extension.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Iran releases US resident from Evin Prison during Trump's deal window — a rare gesture of goodwill
outlets.summary: Shahab Dalili returned to the US via Armenia after 10 years at Evin on espionage charges. Analysts divided on whether the release signals genuine willingness to deal or is a tactical goodwill gesture.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran army: 'Full command over Hormuz — strategic waterway will never return to previous state'
outlets.summary: Army spokesman Akraminia declared Iran has 'full command over the Strait of Hormuz' and it 'will never return to its previous state' — signaling Hormuz leverage is a permanent Iranian strategic asset.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: Araghchi: 'Lack of trust in US is the main obstacle to any diplomatic effort' — Iran rejects US optimism
outlets.summary: Iran's FM issued his sharpest anti-US statement of the war: 'We cannot trust the Americans at all.' Directly contradicts Trump's 'Iran is desperate' framing as the 2-3 day window ticks down.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Iran releases US resident Dalili from Evin in surprise diplomatic move during Trump's deal deadline
outlets.summary: Iran released Shahab Dalili — imprisoned 10 years on espionage charges — and he returned to the US via Armenia. The gesture coincides with Trump's Friday-Sunday deal deadline but Araghchi's comments signal Iran is not making nuclear concessions.
